IT-Email Service

What is E-Mail?

E-mail (Electronic Mail) is the primary service used to conduct internal and external university communication in an official and secure environment. The KHAS e-mail infrastructure is supported by cloud-based security filters and spam protection.

Only the

should be used in all academic and administrative correspondence.

Account Formats

Students:

[email protected]

Academic/Administrative Staff:

[email protected]

The username is determined by Student Affairs or Human Resources and is used in the same way for system logins.

Key Features

High-security

spam and virus filtering

Compatibility with mobile and desktop clients

Calendar, contact list, and task integration

Corporate storage and cloud synchronization support

Prerequisites

Active

Up-to-date password

Internet access and compatible client (Outlook, Gmail, Apple Mail, etc.)

Usage

Log in:

Sign in with your corporate account and password.

Configure the client:

Add your account to your preferred e-mail client.

Official communication:

Use only your KHAS e-mail account for lectures, announcements, and administrative processes.

Security Principles

Do not share

your password with third parties.

Do not open suspicious e-mails or click on links.

Report phishing attempts immediately to IT Support

.

Update your password at regular intervals.

You can open a support request for installation, password, or access issues.

Quick Search

Through this form, you can access news, announcements, faculties and departments, base scores, our academic staff and administrative personnel, and their internal phone numbers.
